module.name: no-query-side-effects
module.description: Disallow side effects in query-style functions (get*/is*/has*/can*/should*); separate query from modifier.

scenarios.name: should allow a query function that only reads and returns a value
scenarios.given: a function named getName that returns a property without mutations

scenarios.name: should allow a non-query function that contains side effects
scenarios.given: a function named updateCount that increments a counter

scenarios.name: should allow a nested inner function with side effects inside a query function
scenarios.given: a get function that contains a nested inner function performing mutations
scenarios.then: no violation is reported as the inner function is a separate scope

scenarios.name: should allow mutating a locally-declared collection to build a return value
scenarios.given: a get function that declares a local Map, populates it, and returns it
scenarios.when: the rule evaluates the mutating call on the local Map
scenarios.then: no violation is reported because the mutated object was declared within the same scope

scenarios.name: should allow mutating a locally-declared array to build a return value
scenarios.given: a get function that declares a local array, pushes items, and returns it
scenarios.when: the rule evaluates the push call on the local array
scenarios.then: no violation is reported because the mutated object is a local collection

scenarios.name: should report an assignment to an outer-scope variable inside a get function
scenarios.given: a function named getCount that reassigns an external variable total
scenarios.when: the rule evaluates the assignment expression

scenarios.name: should report an update expression inside an is function
scenarios.given: a function named isReady that increments an external counter
scenarios.when: the rule evaluates the update expression

scenarios.name: should report a mutating method call on an externally-scoped collection inside a has function
scenarios.given: a function named hasItems that calls push on a parameter-provided array
scenarios.when: the rule evaluates the call expression

scenarios.name: should report delete of an external object property inside a can function
scenarios.given: a function named canProceed that deletes a property on a parameter-passed object
scenarios.when: the rule evaluates the unary delete expression
